name: No guts, no glory
description: TV arrived just in time for the 1956 Melbourne Olympics. The Olympics crystallise ideas of nationalism, athletic heroism, and controversy, all of which have been explored through countless features, documentaries and TV shows.

key_work.title: Melbourne Olympics
key_work.brief_description: After years of waiting, TV was finally broadcast in Australia – just in time for the 1956 Melbourne Olympics, which was aired by all three Melbourne TV stations and was the first Olympic Games outside of Europe and America.

constellations_primary.name: No guts, no glory
constellations_primary.description: TV arrived just in time for the 1956 Melbourne Olympics. The Olympics crystallise ideas of nationalism, athletic heroism, and controversy, all of which have been explored through countless features, documentaries and TV shows.

constellations_other.name: Spectacle
constellations_other.description: The moving image has captured some of history’s biggest moments. From newsreel shorts in cinemas before the show to the arrival of TV, nightly news and the 24-hour news cycle to social media, news has become a spectacle.

start.title: Melbourne Olympics
start.brief_description: After years of waiting, TV was finally broadcast in Australia – just in time for the 1956 Melbourne Olympics, which was aired by all three Melbourne TV stations and was the first Olympic Games outside of Europe and America.

end.title: Olympia Part One: Festival of the Nations
end.brief_description: The First part of Olympia, a documentary about the 1936 Olympic games in Berlin by German Director Leni Riefenstahl. The film played in theaters in 1938 and again in 1952 after the fall of the Nazi Regime.

constellations_other.name: No guts, no glory
constellations_other.description: TV arrived just in time for the 1956 Melbourne Olympics. The Olympics crystallise ideas of nationalism, athletic heroism, and controversy, all of which have been explored through countless features, documentaries and TV shows.

links.description: The Olympic Games have historically offered an opportunity for technological developments in filmmaking, including the motion picture techniques employed by Leni Riefenstahl in her visually dazzling, yet politically controversial, documentary about the 1938 Berlin Olympics.

start.title: Cool Runnings
start.creator_credit: Jon Turteltaub, Grania Preston, Dawn Steel, Walt Disney Pictures, Stephen Marsh, Lynn Siefert, Michael Goldberg, Tommy Swerdlow and Michael Ritchie
start.brief_description: When a Jamaican sprinter is disqualified from the Olympic Games, he enlists the help of a dishonored coach to start the first Jamaican Bobsled Team.

constellations_other.name: No guts, no glory
constellations_other.description: TV arrived just in time for the 1956 Melbourne Olympics. The Olympics crystallise ideas of nationalism, athletic heroism, and controversy, all of which have been explored through countless features, documentaries and TV shows.

start.creator_credit_for_label: Jon Turteltaub, Grania Preston, Dawn Steel, Walt Disney Pictures, Stephen Marsh, Lynn Siefert, Michael Goldberg, Tommy Swerdlow and Michael Ritchie

end.title: The Games
end.brief_description: The Games was an Australian mockumentary television series about the 2000 Summer Olympics in Sydney. The series was originally broadcast on the ABC and had two seasons of 13 episodes each, the first in 1998 and the second in 2000.

constellations_other.name: No guts, no glory
constellations_other.description: TV arrived just in time for the 1956 Melbourne Olympics. The Olympics crystallise ideas of nationalism, athletic heroism, and controversy, all of which have been explored through countless features, documentaries and TV shows.

links.description: In the lead up to the 2000 Sydney Olympics, Australian mockumentary series The Games, starring John Clarke, Brian Dawe and Gina Riley, satirised the internal politics, bureaucratic ineptitude and corruption of the Sydney Organising Committee.

start.title: Icarus
start.creator_credit: Bryan Fogel, Dan Cogan, David Fialkow, Jim Swartz, Alex Productions, Diamond Docs, Impact Partners, Lagralane Group, The, Rise Films, Jon Bertain, Mark Monroe and Timothy Rode
start.brief_description: While investigating the furtive world of illegal doping in sports, director Bryan Fogel connects with renegade Russian scientist Dr. Grigory Rodchenkov—a pillar of his country’s “anti-doping” program. Over dozens of Skype calls, urine samples, and badly administered hormone injections, Fogel and Rodchenkov grow closer despite shocking allegations that place Rodchenkov at the center of Russia’s state-sponsored Olympic doping program.

end.title: I, Tonya
end.creator_credit: Craig Gillespie, Jennifer Johnson, Bryan Unkeless, Margot Robbie, Steven Rogers, Tom Ackerley, Clubhouse Pictures, LuckyChap Entertainment, Jade Healy, Justin David Burton and Pierina Bustamante

video_links.name: I, TONYA [Official Trailer] – In Theaters Now
I, TONYA [Trailer] Redband Trailer – In Theaters Now

end.brief_description: Competitive ice skater Tonya Harding rises amongst the ranks at the U.S. Figure Skating Championships, but her future in the sport is thrown into doubt when her ex-husband intervenes.

links.description: Competition and controversy run high in the Olympic Games. Loosely based on real events, I, Tonya, chronicles the life of figure skater Tonya Harding and her connection to an attack on rival Nancy Kerrigan prior to the Winter Olympics.

end.title: Tokyo Olympiad
end.creator_credit: Kon Ichikawa, Taguchi Suketaro, TOHO, Tokyo Olympic Film Association, Shuntaro Tanikawa, Natto Wada and Yoshio Shirasaka
end.brief_description: This impressionistic portrait of the 1964 Tokyo Summer Olympics pays as much attention to the crowds and workers as it does to the actual competitive events. Highlights include an epic pole-vaulting match between West Germany and America, and the final marathon race through Tokyo’s streets.

constellations_other.name: No guts, no glory
constellations_other.description: TV arrived just in time for the 1956 Melbourne Olympics. The Olympics crystallise ideas of nationalism, athletic heroism, and controversy, all of which have been explored through countless features, documentaries and TV shows.

end.creator_credit_for_label: Kon Ichikawa, Taguchi Suketaro, TOHO, Tokyo Olympic Film Association, Shuntaro Tanikawa, Natto Wada and Yoshio Shirasaka

links.description: The 1964 Tokyo Olympics were also captured with impressive filmmaking techniques in the documentary Tokyo Olympiad, directed by Kon Ichikawa, who used multiple cameras, telephoto lenses and slow motion to capture the drama and poetry of the athletes and crowds.

start.title: Tokyo Olympiad

end.title: Munich
end.creator_credit: Steven Spielberg, Joanna Johnston, Barry Mendel, Colin Wilson, Kathleen Kennedy, Alliance Atlantis Communications, Amblin Entertainment, Barry Mendel Productions, DreamWorks Pictures, Peninsula Films, The Kennedy/Marshall Company, Universal Pictures, Rick Carter, Eric Roth and Tony Kushner
end.headline_credit: Canada, France and United States of America, 2005
end.brief_description: During the 1972 Olympic Games in Munich, eleven Israeli athletes are taken hostage and murdered by a Palestinian terrorist group known as Black September. In retaliation, the Israeli government recruits a group of Mossad agents to track down and execute those responsible for the attack.

constellations_other.name: No guts, no glory
constellations_other.description: TV arrived just in time for the 1956 Melbourne Olympics. The Olympics crystallise ideas of nationalism, athletic heroism, and controversy, all of which have been explored through countless features, documentaries and TV shows.

end.headline_credit_for_label: Canada, France and United States of America, 2005

links.description: Another film based on real events is Munich, a historical drama exploring the aftermath and retaliation to the terrorist attack at the 1972 Olympic Games, which resulted in the death of 11 athletes from the Israeli team.

links.description: Also loosely based on real events, comedy Cool Runnings follows the Jamaican bobsled team as they prepare for the 1988 Winter Olympics despite never having seen the snow.
